title: echium creticum subsp. macranthum
intro: Echium creticum subsp. macranthum, commonly known as the Cretan bugloss, is a plant species in the borage family native to the island of Crete in Greece. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Echium creticum subsp. macranthum are often used in traditional medicine, and it can be found in various forms, including capsules, tinctures, and teas.
